retrieved_chunks.content:
a suspense date, as well as pending issues originally established as future diaries, but which have passed their diary suspense date and have become regular pending issues with a date of claim and a new suspense date. (See Section 6.09 below.) For ESU, these include pending issues established with a date of claim and a suspense date, and future diaries nearing their diary suspense date, since there is no W60 screen for ESU issues. Obtain a detail listing of cases from Column A weekly to assure timely processing of issues.

b. Columns B through G display issues which have already matured and fall in the time categories listed. The statistical information shown on the screen progresses from Column A to Column G as the individual issues mature and become past due. Supervisors should obtain detail listings from these columns at their discretion to identify and resolve problems with particular end products or organizational units.

c. For each pending issue, the W-50 detail prints show file number, payee, stub name, EPC, benefit, date of claim, suspense date, and folder location. To obtain a detail print, see Paragraph 6.02(c).


Go To Top

6.09  MATURING DIARY ISSUES - W60 SCREEN

The Maturing Diary Issue screen W60 also has two purposes. Column A identifies diaries which are reaching their suspense date and will now require action. Columns B through F and the TOTAL ISSUES column display the number of issues that have been diaried in the BDN system for future action or establishment of an actual pending issue. This information enables management to plan in advance for this segment of the workload. This screen shows only authorization issues, not ESU issues. Pending ESU future diaries which are not yet approaching their suspense dates are accessible only by the WINQ (Work INQuiry) command. See Section 6.08 for maturing ESU future diaries. See Figure 6.8 for a representation of the W60 screen layout.

results.query: How many years of service is excluded for a ROTC-commissioned officer under Chapter 33?
results.expected_answer: 4 years

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service
retrieved_chunks.content:
c. Determining excluded period dates

Because chapter 33 is a title 38 benefit, the VA is responsible for prescription of regulations which govern administration of the benefit. Accordingly, VA has determined a commission under the authority of 2107(b) of Title 10 will incur a four-year exclusion period under chapter 33. The VIS application currently reports exclusion dates based on data reported by DoD, including the accession source code, in the “Excluded Obligated Periods of Service” table. The dates which appear in this table are derived data and not authoritative. The reported dates may be used as a guide for determining the exclusion period, but may contain clearly erroneous data. While the VA must rely on DoD data to make these determinations, the DoD does not determine applicability of an exclusion, nor the dates of an exclusion. VA is the sole authority for determining the applicable dates for the exclusion period, based on facts found.

Determine the exclusion period when 2107(b) is an issue by using the following process:

1. Commissioned as an officer in regular component – The exclusion period for a solider commissioned as an officer in a regular component begins on the entrance on duty date of the service period which began due to commission and ends after four years. Service begins accruing towards eligibility on the four-year anniversary of commission.

A solider who is member of a Guard or Reserve component while in the ROTC program, and who subsequently receives a commission as an officer in a regular component and has a period of delayed entry between their commission date and entrance on duty in the regular component, should have their exclusion period begin on the entrance on duty date in the regular component and end after four years. Service begins accruing towards eligibility on the four-year anniversary of their entrance on duty.

Example 1: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a regular component on May 23, 2019. They were a member of a Reserve component from September 27, 2017, to May 22, 2019, while participating in the ROTC program. They entered the regular component on May 23, 2019. The exclusion period is May 23, 2019, to May 22, 2023. Service begins accruing on May 23, 2019.

Example 2: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a regular component on May 23, 2019. They were a member of a Reserve component from September 27, 2017, to September 20, 2019, while participating in the ROTC program. They entered the regular component on September 21, 2019, after a period of delayed entry. The exclusion period is September 21, 2019, to September 20, 2023. Service begins accruing on September 21, 2023.

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service
heading_path: 5.02 Periods of Active Duty Excluded from Eligibility Determinations
retrieved_chunks.content:
Scenario

Example ROTC:  A veteran is discharged from service with a R.O.T.C. obligated period. That period of R.O.T.C. must be excluded from his/her chapter 33 benefits eligibility. The record reflects service on active duty as follows:

September 1, 2004 to September 2, 2009. Since four years of service must be excluded. The processor will enter the service dates from September 1, 2004, to September 2, 2009, and then enter September 1, 2004, to August 31, 2008, as excluded service. Eligibility does not begin until the day after their four-year commitment has been completed. The eligible period of service for chapter 33 benefits would be September 1, 2008, to September 2, 2009.

Example Service Academy:

A West Point graduate, graduates on May 27, 2003, was commissioned on May 27, 2003, and served through May 26, 2008. The individual's academy commitment ended on May 27, 2008. The five year excluded period runs from May 27, 2003 through May 26, 2008. The first date eligibility for a period of qualifying service may be counted is May 27, 2008.

Title 10 USC 4348(a)(2)(B) for USMA, Title 10 USC 6959(a)(2)(B) for United States Naval Academy,  and Title 1010 USC 9348(a)(2)(B) for United States Air Force Academy.“will serve on active duty for at least five years immediately after such appointment”.

 Back to top

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service
heading_path: 5.02 Periods of Active Duty Excluded from Eligibility Determinations
retrieved_chunks.content:
• Obligated service pursuant to being commissioned through a Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) scholarship program under 10 U.S.C. 2107(b). The obligated period is four years.
  • Obligated service pursuant to a commission upon graduating from a Service Academy. The obligated period is five years.

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service
retrieved_chunks.content:
Example 3: A solider enters the regular component as an enlisted member. Subsequently, they enter the ROTC program while on duty and receive a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in the same component.  They entered the regular component on July 1, 2014, participated in the ROTC program while on duty, and were commissioned June 14, 2016. The exclusion period is June 14, 2016, to June 13, 2020. Service performed prior to June 14, 2016, and service performed on or after June 14, 2020, accrue towards the eligibility.

2. Commissioned as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component – The exclusion period for a solider commissioned as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component begins on the commission date and ends after four years. Any active-duty service performed under a qualifying section during the four-year period must be excluded. Active duty performed under a qualifying section on or after the four-year anniversary will accrue towards eligibility.

Example 1: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component on June 5, 2020. They were a member of a Guard or Reserve component from September 20, 2017, to June 4, 2020, while participating in the ROTC program. They became an officer in the Guard or Reserve on June 5, 2020. Any active-duty service performed under a qualifying section from June 5, 2020, to June 4, 2024, must be excluded. Active duty performed under a qualifying section on or after June 5, 2024, will accrue towards eligibility.

 Go to Top

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service
retrieved_chunks.content:
a. Determining Commissioning Statute

A commission received after successful completion of the ROTC program may be under one of three different statutory authorities: section 2014, 2107(b), or 2107a of Title 10. Only a commission under the authority of 2107(b) results in an obligated exclusion period under chapter 33.

To determine the commissioning statute, processors should refer to the “Military Source Accession Code” field, located in the “Excluded Obligated Periods of Service” section of the Veterans Information System (VIS) report. The following table contains the accession codes correlated to ROTC, the commissioning statute of each, and whether an exclusion applies.

If conflicting information is reported, such as exclusion data displayed with an accession code other than one listed above or any other indicator ROTC may be an issue, further development may be required to accurately determine if an exclusion applies.

b. Evaluating ROTC Scholarship Contracts. 

When further development is required to accurately determine the applicability of an exclusion and appropriate exclusion dates, it may be necessary to evaluate the ROTC Scholarship contract and/or orders relating to the commission. The title at the top of each ROTC contract will state whether it is a ROTC Non-scholarship Cadet Contract or a ROTC Scholarship Cadet Contract. ROTC Non-scholarship Cadet Contracts are always under section 2104 of title 10, U.S.C. While the contract may require an individual to fulfill an active-duty service obligation upon receiving his or her commission, this active-duty service should NOT be excluded from qualifying service under chapter 33.

ROTC Scholarship Cadet Contracts can be under either section 2107 or 2107a of title 10, U.S.C. The contract should indicate whether it is under section 2107 or section 2107a.

context_strings:
[Chunk 1] Source: 554400000078114 — Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service Heading: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service > 5.04 ROTC: Scholarship vs. Non-scholarship Contracts

c. Determining excluded period dates

Because chapter 33 is a title 38 benefit, the VA is responsible for prescription of regulations which govern administration of the benefit. Accordingly, VA has determined a commission under the authority of 2107(b) of Title 10 will incur a four-year exclusion period under chapter 33. The VIS application currently reports exclusion dates based on data reported by DoD, including the accession source code, in the “Excluded Obligated Periods of Service” table. The dates which appear in this table are derived data and not authoritative. The reported dates may be used as a guide for determining the exclusion period, but may contain clearly erroneous data. While the VA must rely on DoD data to make these determinations, the DoD does not determine applicability of an exclusion, nor the dates of an exclusion. VA is the sole authority for determining the applicable dates for the exclusion period, based on facts found.

Determine the exclusion period when 2107(b) is an issue by using the following process:

1. Commissioned as an officer in regular component – The exclusion period for a solider commissioned as an officer in a regular component begins on the entrance on duty date of the service period which began due to commission and ends after four years. Service begins accruing towards eligibility on the four-year anniversary of commission.

A solider who is member of a Guard or Reserve component while in the ROTC program, and who subsequently receives a commission as an officer in a regular component and has a period of delayed entry between their commission date and entrance on duty in the regular component, should have their exclusion period begin on the entrance on duty date in the regular component and end after four years. Service begins accruing towards eligibility on the four-year anniversary of their entrance on duty.

Example 1: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a regular component on May 23, 2019. They were a member of a Reserve component from September 27, 2017, to May 22, 2019, while participating in the ROTC program. They entered the regular component on May 23, 2019. The exclusion period is May 23, 2019, to May 22, 2023. Service begins accruing on May 23, 2019.

Example 2: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a regular component on May 23, 2019. They were a member of a Reserve component from September 27, 2017, to September 20, 2019, while participating in the ROTC program. They entered the regular component on September 21, 2019, after a period of delayed entry. The exclusion period is September 21, 2019, to September 20, 2023. Service begins accruing on September 21, 2023.

[Chunk 2] Source: 554400000078114 — Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service Heading: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service > 5.02 Periods of Active Duty Excluded from Eligibility Determinations

Scenario

Example ROTC:  A veteran is discharged from service with a R.O.T.C. obligated period. That period of R.O.T.C. must be excluded from his/her chapter 33 benefits eligibility. The record reflects service on active duty as follows:

September 1, 2004 to September 2, 2009. Since four years of service must be excluded. The processor will enter the service dates from September 1, 2004, to September 2, 2009, and then enter September 1, 2004, to August 31, 2008, as excluded service. Eligibility does not begin until the day after their four-year commitment has been completed. The eligible period of service for chapter 33 benefits would be September 1, 2008, to September 2, 2009.

Example Service Academy:

A West Point graduate, graduates on May 27, 2003, was commissioned on May 27, 2003, and served through May 26, 2008. The individual's academy commitment ended on May 27, 2008. The five year excluded period runs from May 27, 2003 through May 26, 2008. The first date eligibility for a period of qualifying service may be counted is May 27, 2008.

Title 10 USC 4348(a)(2)(B) for USMA, Title 10 USC 6959(a)(2)(B) for United States Naval Academy,  and Title 1010 USC 9348(a)(2)(B) for United States Air Force Academy.“will serve on active duty for at least five years immediately after such appointment”.

 Back to top

[Chunk 3] Source: 554400000078114 — Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service Heading: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service > 5.02 Periods of Active Duty Excluded from Eligibility Determinations
  • Obligated service pursuant to being commissioned through a Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) scholarship program under 10 U.S.C. 2107(b). The obligated period is four years.
  • Obligated service pursuant to a commission upon graduating from a Service Academy. The obligated period is five years.

[Chunk 4] Source: 554400000078114 — Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service Heading: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service > 5.04 ROTC: Scholarship vs. Non-scholarship Contracts

Example 3: A solider enters the regular component as an enlisted member. Subsequently, they enter the ROTC program while on duty and receive a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in the same component.  They entered the regular component on July 1, 2014, participated in the ROTC program while on duty, and were commissioned June 14, 2016. The exclusion period is June 14, 2016, to June 13, 2020. Service performed prior to June 14, 2016, and service performed on or after June 14, 2020, accrue towards the eligibility.

2. Commissioned as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component – The exclusion period for a solider commissioned as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component begins on the commission date and ends after four years. Any active-duty service performed under a qualifying section during the four-year period must be excluded. Active duty performed under a qualifying section on or after the four-year anniversary will accrue towards eligibility.

Example 1: A solider receives a commission under 2107(b) to serve as an officer in a Guard or Reserve component on June 5, 2020. They were a member of a Guard or Reserve component from September 20, 2017, to June 4, 2020, while participating in the ROTC program. They became an officer in the Guard or Reserve on June 5, 2020. Any active-duty service performed under a qualifying section from June 5, 2020, to June 4, 2024, must be excluded. Active duty performed under a qualifying section on or after June 5, 2024, will accrue towards eligibility.

 Go to Top

[Chunk 5] Source: 554400000078114 — Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service Heading: Sub-Chapter 5 Eligibility Excluded Service > 5.04 ROTC: Scholarship vs. Non-scholarship Contracts

a. Determining Commissioning Statute

A commission received after successful completion of the ROTC program may be under one of three different statutory authorities: section 2014, 2107(b), or 2107a of Title 10. Only a commission under the authority of 2107(b) results in an obligated exclusion period under chapter 33.

To determine the commissioning statute, processors should refer to the “Military Source Accession Code” field, located in the “Excluded Obligated Periods of Service” section of the Veterans Information System (VIS) report. The following table contains the accession codes correlated to ROTC, the commissioning statute of each, and whether an exclusion applies.

If conflicting information is reported, such as exclusion data displayed with an accession code other than one listed above or any other indicator ROTC may be an issue, further development may be required to accurately determine if an exclusion applies.

b. Evaluating ROTC Scholarship Contracts. 

When further development is required to accurately determine the applicability of an exclusion and appropriate exclusion dates, it may be necessary to evaluate the ROTC Scholarship contract and/or orders relating to the commission. The title at the top of each ROTC contract will state whether it is a ROTC Non-scholarship Cadet Contract or a ROTC Scholarship Cadet Contract. ROTC Non-scholarship Cadet Contracts are always under section 2104 of title 10, U.S.C. While the contract may require an individual to fulfill an active-duty service obligation upon receiving his or her commission, this active-duty service should NOT be excluded from qualifying service under chapter 33.

ROTC Scholarship Cadet Contracts can be under either section 2107 or 2107a of title 10, U.S.C. The contract should indicate whether it is under section 2107 or section 2107a.
results.answer:
Based on the provided context, **four years** of service is excluded for an officer commissioned through an ROTC scholarship program under 10 U.S.C. 2107(b).

As stated in the policy: *"Obligated service pursuant to being commissioned through a Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) scholarship program under 10 U.S.C. 2107(b). **The obligated period is four years.**"* [3]

It is important to note that this exclusion applies **only** to commissions under the authority of **2107(b)** of Title 10. Commissions under sections 2104 or 2107a do **not** result in an obligated exclusion period under Chapter 33. [5]

retrieved_chunks.title: Sub-Chapter 2. Veterans
heading_path: 2.02 Length of Service and Separation Reasons2.02 Length of Service and Separation Reasons
retrieved_chunks.content:
• A period of title 10 service authorized under a section other than 688, 12301(a), 12301(d), 12301(g), 12301(h), 12302, 12304, 12304a, or 12304b
  • A period of title 14 service authorized under a section other than section 3713 (formerly section 712)
  • A period of title 32 service other than:
    1. Full-time service as an Active Guard Reservist (AGR) for the purposes of organizing, administering, recruiting, instructing, or training the National Guard, or;
    2. Full-time service in the National Guard under section 502(f) of title 32 when authorized by the President or the Secretary of Defense for the purpose of responding to a national emergency declared by the President and supported by Federal funds
  • A period of title 42 service authorized under any section
  • A period of service of an officer commissioned after completion of a Reserve Officers’ Training Corps (ROTC) course under section 2107(b) of title 10 *
  • A period of service of an officer commissioned after graduation from a military service academy under section 7448, 8459, or 9448 of title 10 *
  • A period of service of an officer commissioned after graduation from the Coast Guard Academy under section 1925 (formerly 182) of title 14 after January 3, 2011 *
  • A period of service terminated due to defective enlistment or induction, based on the individual being a minor, an erroneous enlistment or induction, or a defective enlistment agreement
  • A period of service counted for purposes of repayment of an education loan under chapter 109 of title 10
  • A period of Selected Reserve service used to establish eligibility under the Montgomery GI Bill – Active Duty (MGIB or chapter 30), Montgomery GI Bill – Selected Reserve (MGIB-SR or chapter 1606), or the Reserve Education Assistance Program (REAP or chapter 1607) **
  • A period of service in the Armed Forces, beginning on or after August 1, 2011, which was used to establish eligibility under the Montgomery GI Bill – Active Duty (MGIB or chapter 30), Montgomery GI Bill – Selected Reserve (MGIB-SR or chapter 1606), or the Reserve Education Assistance Program (REAP or chapter 1607) **

results.answer:
Based on the provided context, the excluded period for a Service Academy graduate commissioned under Title 10 is **five years**.

As stated in the policy: *"Obligated service pursuant to a commission upon graduating from a Service Academy. The obligated period is five years."* [1]

The relevant statutory authorities for this exclusion are:
- **Title 10 USC 4348(a)(2)(B)** for the U.S. Military Academy (West Point)
- **Title 10 USC 6959(a)(2)(B)** for the U.S. Naval Academy
- **Title 10 USC 9348(a)(2)(B)** for the U.S. Air Force Academy

These statutes require that graduates "will serve on active duty for at least five years immediately after such appointment." [2]

**Example:** A West Point graduate commissioned on May 27, 2003, would have an excluded period running from **May 27, 2003 through May 26, 2008**, with the first date of eligibility for qualifying service beginning **May 27, 2008**. [2]

results.query: What is the maximum percentage of tuition and fees covered by accelerated payment under Chapter 30?
results.expected_answer: 60% of established tuition and fees for one term

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment
retrieved_chunks.content:
An accelerated payment is a lump sum payment of a maximum of 60 percent of the established tuition and fees for an individual's enrollment for a term, quarter, or semester in an approved program of education leading to employment in a high technology industry.  In the case of a program of education not offered on a term, quarter, or semester basis, the accelerated payment is a lump sum payment of a maximum of 60 percent of the established tuition and fees for the entire program (38 CFR 21.7020(b)(51)). 

NOTE 1:  There is no provision for paying less than 60 percent of tuition and fees based on a claimant's request. VA would only pay less than 60 percent if the claimant had insufficient remaining entitlement or reached his or her delimiting date during the enrollment period for which accelerated payment was requested.  

NOTE 2:  Accelerated payment is a completely separate benefit from lump sum payment (see pt. V, par. 1.25 for lump sum payment.)  

Go to Top

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment
retrieved_chunks.content:
If the tuition and fees do not exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display the message “Not Eligible for Accelerated Payment.”  Pay regular chapter 30 benefits and send the claimant a disallowance letter (see PCGL DIS-20).

If the total charges exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display one of these two messages:

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment
heading_path: 11.06 RESERVED(Change Date:  April 28, 2008)11.06 RESERVED(Change Date:  April 28, 2008)
retrieved_chunks.content:
1.  Determine the actual tuition and fees (see subpar. b) for the term or other enrollment period and also determine the established tuition and fees (see subpar. a ) for that period. 

2. Determine the length of the enrollment period and the training time in the usual manner.

3. Determine the basic chapter 30 monthly rate for the individual's training time during the enrollment.   This basic rate would include chapter 30 kickers (but not chapter 1606 kickers), payment for dependents, and the additional amount based on the $600 additional contribution.  (The phrase "chapter 30 kickers" includes chapter 32 kickers for individuals who previously qualified for chapter 32 but elected to convert to chapter 30 benefits.)  For the effect of rate increases during the enrollment period, see paragraph 11.17. 

4.  Divide the actual tuition and fees by the length of the course expressed in months and fractions of a month. 

5.  If the result in step 4 exceeds 200 percent of the basic rate payable (from step 3), then the individual is eligible for accelerated payment (assuming the requirements of pars. 11.07 and 11.08 are met).   

6. The accelerated payment is 60 percent of the established tuition and fees, or a lesser amount if the individual has insufficient remaining entitlement or the individual's delimiting date occurs within the enrollment period (see subpars. e and f).

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment
retrieved_chunks.content:
f the revised total charges do not exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display the message “No” in the “Eligible” field. Pay regular chapter 30 benefits from the date of reduction and send the claimant an amended award letter. 

If the revised total charges continue to exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display the message “Yes” in the “Eligible” field.

The spreadsheet will display revised award and certification data that must be entered in the Chapter 30 PC to adjust the claimant’s award for both situations described above.

3. Terminated Award with Mitigating Circumstances spreadsheet.  Use this spreadsheet when a claimant terminates his or her enrollment with acceptable mitigating circumstances.  Minimal entries are required to determine a claimant’s revised accelerated payment amount and generate information needed for award processing. The steps to complete this spreadsheet are as follows.

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment
retrieved_chunks.content:
• Eligible for 60 percent of Course Cost. The individual has enough remaining entitlement to receive the full 60 percent of the total charges.

  • Eligible for Remaining Entitlement. The individual is only eligible to receive the amount of the charges that are equal to the individual's remaining entitlement.  In some instances, it may be to the individual's advantage to receive regular chapter 30 benefits. The spreadsheet has a field that will tell you whether or not it is to the individual’s advantage to receive an accelerated payment. See PCGL Letter DEV-23 letter to solicit the veteran's election in this situation.

context_strings:
[Chunk 1] Source: 554400000076342 — Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment Heading: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment > 11.02  ACCELERATED PAYMENT DEFINED

An accelerated payment is a lump sum payment of a maximum of 60 percent of the established tuition and fees for an individual's enrollment for a term, quarter, or semester in an approved program of education leading to employment in a high technology industry.  In the case of a program of education not offered on a term, quarter, or semester basis, the accelerated payment is a lump sum payment of a maximum of 60 percent of the established tuition and fees for the entire program (38 CFR 21.7020(b)(51)). 

NOTE 1:  There is no provision for paying less than 60 percent of tuition and fees based on a claimant's request. VA would only pay less than 60 percent if the claimant had insufficient remaining entitlement or reached his or her delimiting date during the enrollment period for which accelerated payment was requested.  

NOTE 2:  Accelerated payment is a completely separate benefit from lump sum payment (see pt. V, par. 1.25 for lump sum payment.)  

Go to Top

[Chunk 2] Source: 554400000076342 — Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment Heading: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment > SUBCHAPTER II. CLAIMS PROCESSING PROCEDURES > 11.15 ACCELERATED PAYMENT WORKBOOK > a. Excel workbook.

If the tuition and fees do not exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display the message “Not Eligible for Accelerated Payment.”  Pay regular chapter 30 benefits and send the claimant a disallowance letter (see PCGL DIS-20).

If the total charges exceed 200 percent of the total basic educational assistance otherwise payable, the spreadsheet will display one of these two messages:

[Chunk 3] Source: 554400000076342 — Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment Heading: Part 5: Chapter 11 - Accelerated Payment > 11.06 RESERVED(Change Date:  April 28, 2008)11.06 RESERVED(Change Date:  April 28, 2008) > 11.09  CALCULATING THE ACCELERATED PAYMENT > c. Accelerated Payment amount.

1.  Determine the actual tuition and fees (see subpar. b) for the term or other enrollment period and also determine the established tuition and fees (see subpar. a ) for that period. 

2. Determine the length of the enrollment period and the training time in the usual manner.

3. Determine the basic chapter 30 monthly rate for the individual's training time during the enrollment.   This basic rate would include chapter 30 kickers (but not chapter 1606 kickers), payment for dependents, and the additional amount based on the $600 additional contribution.  (The phrase "chapter 30 kickers" includes chapter 32 kickers for individuals who previously qualified for chapter 32 but elected to convert to chapter 30 benefits.)  For the effect of rate increases during the enrollment period, see paragraph 11.17. 

4.  Divide the actual tuition and fees by the length of the course expressed in months and fractions of a month. 

5.  If the result in step 4 exceeds 200 percent of the basic rate payable (from step 3), then the individual is eligible for accelerated payment (assuming the requirements of pars. 11.07 and 11.08 are met).   

6. The accelerated payment is 60 percent of the established tuition and fees, or a lesser amount if the individual has insufficient remaining entitlement or the individual's delimiting date occurs within the enrollment period (see subpars. e and f).

results.answer:
Based on the provided context, the maximum percentage of tuition and fees covered by accelerated payment is **60 percent**.

Specifically, "an accelerated payment is a lump sum payment of a maximum of 60 percent of the established tuition and fees for an individual's enrollment for a term, quarter, or semester in an approved program of education leading to employment in a high technology industry." [1]

It is also important to note that **there is no provision for paying less than 60 percent** based on a claimant's request — VA would only pay less than 60 percent if the claimant had insufficient remaining entitlement or reached their delimiting date during the enrollment period. [1]

results.query: What happens to OJT housing allowance after the first year of a Chapter 33 apprenticeship?
results.expected_answer: It drops from 100% to 80% of E-5 BAH in the second 6 months, then 60% in the third 6 months, 40% in the fourth, and 20% after 24 months

retrieved_chunks.title: Chapter 33 Sub-Chapter 4.3 Housing Allowance
heading_path: 3.05 Housing Benefit for OJT/Apprenticeship3.05 Housing Benefit for OJT/Apprenticeship
retrieved_chunks.content:
Individuals pursuing an approved OJT or apprenticeship program are paid a monthly allowance (similar to the MHA paid for institutional training) as follows:

  • First 6 months: A monthly amount equal to the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Second 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 80% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Third 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 60% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Fourth 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 40% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Over 24 months: A monthly amount equal to 20% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)

results.answer:
Based on the provided context, after the first year (first 6 months + second 6 months) of a Chapter 33 OJT/apprenticeship program, the monthly housing allowance continues to decrease on a step-down schedule as follows:

- **Second 6 months** (months 7–12): **80%** of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents
- **Third 6 months** (months 13–18): **60%** of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents
- **Fourth 6 months** (months 19–24): **40%** of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents
- **Over 24 months**: **20%** of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents

All amounts are based on the BAH rate where the **employer is located**, and are prorated based on the individual's benefit level and/or if training is less than 120 hours per month. [1]

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 4: Chapter 13 - Miscellaneous Award Issues
heading_path: SUBCHAPTER VIII.  PAYMENT OF ATTORNEY FEES FROM PAST-DUE BENEFITS
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1)  When attorney fees are potentially payable for a chapter 1606 case, before sending the case to BVA for a determination on the amount of attorney fees payable, Adjudication should prepare a VA Form 22-1997 for the past-due benefits period and for any recurring chapter 1606 benefits to which the claimant is entitled.  Twenty percent of any retroactive benefits due must be withheld pending a BVA decision in regard to the amount of attorney fees payable.

(2)  Adjudication will use an OF 41 or local overprint to refer the education folder and hard copy award to the Finance activity with the following statement:  "Please compute retroactive amount which will be generated by award of (enter date of hard copy award).  Also, compute 20 percent of the retroactive amount due".  Send the education folder to the Finance activity for necessary action after taking the following award action:

(i)  No Master Record.  Process a BDN award covering both the past due benefits period and any future period for which payments are payable.  Complete the address segment to make payment to the agent cashier including information to assist the agent cashier to identify the payment (i. e., AC John Doe 123456789 ATTY FEES).  Diary the case to ensure that payment is made (after full file pass).  Check the TINQ (Treasury Inquiry) screen to ensure that payment has been made.  Then process another award to change the address to the current mailing address of the claimant.

(ii)  Active Master Record.  Process a BDN award to change the address to the name of the agent cashier.  Complete the address segment to make payment to the agent cashier including information to assist the agent cashier to identify the payment  (i. e., AC John Doe 123456789 ATTY FEES).  Have this amended award cover any retroactive periods.  Diary the case to ensure that payment is made (after full file pass).  Check the TINQ (Treasury Inquiry) screen to ensure that payment has been made.  Then process another award to change the address to the current mailing address of the claimant.  Exercise care that this action does not interfere with the delivery of current benefits due the claimant.

(iii)  Terminated Master Record.  Follow the procedures in subparagraph (a) above.

NOTE:  See part VIII, chapter 4, for instructions on awards that require out-of-system processing.  The agent cashier should also receive all payments from any out-of-system awards.

retrieved_chunks.title: Part XI: Chapter 9-Out-of-System Award Processing
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1) If the claimant is requesting benefits for any enrollment period(s) paid under Chapter 1606, before adjusting the 1606 record the VCE should first capture the M01, M21, M22, and M23 screens covering the period being terminated. The VCE should use the STOP command and reason code 30 back to the first date from which Chapter 1607 is payable. It is important the BDN letter be suppressed when the Chapter 1606 award is terminated. The STOP command will create an overpayment (or A/R) in BDN; which, in most cases, will be a debt on paper only.

(2) If a debt is created, the VCE must enter the total dollar amount of the overpayment on the spreadsheet under “Previous Ch 1606 Award”; the spreadsheet calculates the balance due the claimant under Chapter 1607 and displays this amount as the “Net Award Due”. If the claimant previously received Chapter 1606 for the same enrollments now being paid under Chapter 1607, the removal of the duplicated periods will also restore the claimant’s entitlement under Chapter 1606. The amount of “original entitlement” input on the spreadsheet for Chapter 1607 must be the claimant’s amount of entitlement AFTER adjusting the Chapter 1606 record.

Example: A claimant used 15 months of Chapter 1606 entitlement. Five months of this is now restored because of award adjustment to pay Chapter 1607. The claimant now has used 10 months under Chapter 1606. Therefore, he should receive a full 36 months under Chapter 1607 (because 36 months of Ch. 1607 plus 10 months under Chapter 1606 is under the 48-month maximum for all benefits). On the Award Period Worksheet section, under “Entitlement”, "Orig Months" entered will be 36.

(3) If the claimant had an existing Chapter 1606 debt prior to this adjustment, the existing debt should be handled one of two ways:

(a) If the debt was created within the last 30 days, the claimant is still within his or her due process period; that debt must be noted prior to stopping the Chapter 1606 award and be subtracted from the new total debt created in BDN by the STOP action before inputting the dollar amount in the “Previous Ch 1606 Award” field on the spreadsheet. Since BDN will add any existing debt to the one being created, it’s important that the amount entered under “Previous Ch 1606 Award” only be the debt created by the Stop action just completed.

(b) If the claimant is beyond his 30-day due process period, include the total debt amount under “Previous Ch 1606 Award”. This action will cause the Chapter 1607 award to recoup the whole debt.

Note: If benefits under Chapters 30, 31, 32, or 35 have been paid for Chapter 1607 eligibility periods, do not adjust the awards for these benefit types. Do not authorize Chapter 1607 for these periods. Once the Chapter 1607 BDN system is installed, VCEs may adjust these benefit records and make retroactive awards in the Chapter 1607 BDN system. It is anticipated that the vast majority of the previous awards will be under Chapter 1606 as opposed to one of the other benefits. VCEs should set aside cases requiring termination of benefits other than Chapter 1606. Identify them in TIMS by entering “1607 OTH" in the facility code field.

(1) date of Chapter 1607 eligibility (from DMDC);

(5)  enrollment periods reported on enrollment certifications;

(7)  periods of ineligibility for Chapter 1607 due to not being in Selected Reserve (or IRR) as reported by DMDC;

(8) enrollment periods in multiple fiscal years requiring use of multiple rate tables.

Example 1:  Background: Army Reserve member is called up November 2, 2004 and serves until November 2, 2005 in a DoD approved contingency. The VA REAP website confirms those dates in the Activation Begin Date and Activation End Date fields. The record shows a Status Code of MA (member eligible) in the appropriate block. (See Appendix B for detail on the VA REAP website.) During that period of service, the servicemember did not take any courses, but was eligible for the 40% after 90 consecutive days of active duty.

The servicemember was eligible for Chapter 1606 before being called to active service, but did not have a Chapter 1606 kicker. Upon separation, this individual starts attending classes at the local university and sends in his (he has never made a claim to VA) VA Form 22-1990 claiming Chapter 1607.

Action: In this case, the VCE will ensure the DMDC screen shows the necessary information to process the claim. The VCE will also review any TIMS information and check his 30D and DOD screens. In this case, eligibility is established. The 60% rate level is payable from November 2,

context_strings:
[Chunk 1] Source: 554400000073684 — Part 4: Chapter 13 - Miscellaneous Award Issues Heading: Part 4: Chapter 13 - Miscellaneous Award Issues > SUBCHAPTER VIII.  PAYMENT OF ATTORNEY FEES FROM PAST-DUE BENEFITS > 13.34  CHAPTER 1606 PROCEDURES > a.  Adjudication Actions

(1)  When attorney fees are potentially payable for a chapter 1606 case, before sending the case to BVA for a determination on the amount of attorney fees payable, Adjudication should prepare a VA Form 22-1997 for the past-due benefits period and for any recurring chapter 1606 benefits to which the claimant is entitled.  Twenty percent of any retroactive benefits due must be withheld pending a BVA decision in regard to the amount of attorney fees payable.

(2)  Adjudication will use an OF 41 or local overprint to refer the education folder and hard copy award to the Finance activity with the following statement:  "Please compute retroactive amount which will be generated by award of (enter date of hard copy award).  Also, compute 20 percent of the retroactive amount due".  Send the education folder to the Finance activity for necessary action after taking the following award action:

(i)  No Master Record.  Process a BDN award covering both the past due benefits period and any future period for which payments are payable.  Complete the address segment to make payment to the agent cashier including information to assist the agent cashier to identify the payment (i. e., AC John Doe 123456789 ATTY FEES).  Diary the case to ensure that payment is made (after full file pass).  Check the TINQ (Treasury Inquiry) screen to ensure that payment has been made.  Then process another award to change the address to the current mailing address of the claimant.

(ii)  Active Master Record.  Process a BDN award to change the address to the name of the agent cashier.  Complete the address segment to make payment to the agent cashier including information to assist the agent cashier to identify the payment  (i. e., AC John Doe 123456789 ATTY FEES).  Have this amended award cover any retroactive periods.  Diary the case to ensure that payment is made (after full file pass).  Check the TINQ (Treasury Inquiry) screen to ensure that payment has been made.  Then process another award to change the address to the current mailing address of the claimant.  Exercise care that this action does not interfere with the delivery of current benefits due the claimant.

(iii)  Terminated Master Record.  Follow the procedures in subparagraph (a) above.

NOTE:  See part VIII, chapter 4, for instructions on awards that require out-of-system processing.  The agent cashier should also receive all payments from any out-of-system awards.

(1) If the claimant is requesting benefits for any enrollment period(s) paid under Chapter 1606, before adjusting the 1606 record the VCE should first capture the M01, M21, M22, and M23 screens covering the period being terminated. The VCE should use the STOP command and reason code 30 back to the first date from which Chapter 1607 is payable. It is important the BDN letter be suppressed when the Chapter 1606 award is terminated. The STOP command will create an overpayment (or A/R) in BDN; which, in most cases, will be a debt on paper only.

(2) If a debt is created, the VCE must enter the total dollar amount of the overpayment on the spreadsheet under “Previous Ch 1606 Award”; the spreadsheet calculates the balance due the claimant under Chapter 1607 and displays this amount as the “Net Award Due”. If the claimant previously received Chapter 1606 for the same enrollments now being paid under Chapter 1607, the removal of the duplicated periods will also restore the claimant’s entitlement under Chapter 1606. The amount of “original entitlement” input on the spreadsheet for Chapter 1607 must be the claimant’s amount of entitlement AFTER adjusting the Chapter 1606 record.

Example: A claimant used 15 months of Chapter 1606 entitlement. Five months of this is now restored because of award adjustment to pay Chapter 1607. The claimant now has used 10 months under Chapter 1606. Therefore, he should receive a full 36 months under Chapter 1607 (because 36 months of Ch. 1607 plus 10 months under Chapter 1606 is under the 48-month maximum for all benefits). results.query: What QA scorecard questions cover payment accuracy in VA education claims?
results.expected_answer: Questions 8 through 10

retrieved_chunks.title: CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE
retrieved_chunks.content:
Improving benefits and services delivery to Veterans, service members, and eligible beneficiaries remains a priority goal for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). QA (Quality Assurance) is a quality measurement program used by Education Service to ensure the quality of education actions by education claims processing employees at the two Regional Processing Offices (RPOs). Education Service will conduct QA reviews of education work monthly for each RPO. Data from QA reviews will be used to compute payment accuracy rates, customer service accuracy and evaluate individual station quality levels.

In addition, QA is a quality measurement program used by Education Service to assess the quality of actions and service given by Education Call Center agents at the National Education Call Center (ECC) located at the Muskogee RPO. Quality monitoring using the scorecard and the evaluator’s expertise provides VBA with a continuous improvement process that supports its primary mission.

SUBCHAPTER II. NATIONAL QUALITY ASSURANCE - CLAIMS PROCESSING

retrieved_chunks.title: CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE
retrieved_chunks.content:
5. Payment. Questions eight through ten in the QA scorecard concern payment.

  • This section is used to determine the accuracy of payments made to beneficiaries as well as facilities. This includes issues involving their enrollment, payment delivery method (EFT or check), fiscal transaction, and manual payments. This section also reviews whether school and program approval were verified before payments were issued.

retrieved_chunks.title: CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE
retrieved_chunks.content:
4. Entitlement. Question seven in the QA scorecard concerns entitlement.

  • This section concerns the accuracy of the number of months and days of entitlement granted to a beneficiary. This includes all previously used entitlement being properly accounted.

retrieved_chunks.title: CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE
retrieved_chunks.content:
3. Eligibility. Question six in the QA scorecard concerns eligibility.

  • This section is used to determine if the eligibility determination was accurate. This includes service information such as qualifying service dates, character of discharge, and separation reason. As well as relinquished benefit, delimiting date, alternative elections, and others.

retrieved_chunks.title: CHAPTER 2. QUALITY ASSURANCE
retrieved_chunks.content:
6. Notification. Questions 11 through 13 in the QA scorecard concern notification.

  • This section concerns correspondence and notification letters. This includes determining that the appropriate letter was sent, the accuracy of the information within the letter (including name and address), and if the proper recipients were notified.

results.query: What are the 7 categories on the M22-3 QA claims processing scorecard?
results.expected_answer: The 7 categories are: (1) Data Integrity (Questions 1-3) — claim type, date of claim, TIMS token; (2) Development (Questions 4-5) — proper development criteria, claim controlled; (3) Eligibility (Question 6) — service dates, character of discharge, separation reason, delimiting date, alternative elections; (4) Entitlement (Question 7) — months/days accuracy, previously used entitlement; (5) Payment (Questions 8-10) — enrollment accuracy, payment delivery method, fiscal transaction, school/program approval; (6) Notification (Questions 11-13) — appropriate letter sent, accuracy of information, proper recipients; (7) Systems Compliance (Questions 14-16) — TIMS documentation, BDN, LTS systems, file status, authorization status, appropriate signatures. There are also Administrative Questions that do not count toward the National Accuracy Rate.

results.query: What are the five VADIR web service operations available through VSCH33Service?WSDL?
results.expected_answer: getPerson, getContactInfo, findPeopleByCriteria, getServicePeriods, getKickerInfo

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
This interface does not require any transaction types.
Chapter 33 LTS provides the initial data exchange through a WSDL:  
VSCH33Service?wsdl has several operations where:  
- a. Chapter 33 LTS provides identity traits (SN) to fetch the Veteran information from the VADIR findPeopleByCriteria operation. VADIR returns one or more records with Demographic Information.
- b. Chapter 33 LTS provides EDIPI to the VADIR getContactInfo operation to fetch complete Demographic Information.
- c. Chapter 33 LTS provides EDIPI to the VADIR getPerson operation to fetch complete Person Information.
- d. Chapter 33 LTS provides EDIPI to the VADIR getServicePeriods operation to fetch ServicePeriod and education-specific Information.
- e. Chapter 33 LTS provides EDIPI to the VADIR getKickerInfo operation to fetch KickerInfo Response Information.  
Two new fields, callStatus & callStatusNote, have been added to all Response objects:  
- a. callStatus field contains either 'Success' or 'Error' with a numerical error code.
- b. callStatusNote field provides a text description of the error.  
Please refer to Section 6.1.1 for additional details on callStatus and callStatusNote.

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
The following qualification methods will be used to verify that requirements have been met:  
-  Demonstration: Ensuring that the VSCH33Service?WSDL is accessible at end points as listed in Section 5, demonstrating that the service is properly deployed and accessible from requesting entities.
-  Test: Once the accessibility of endpoints is established, run through the SOAP UI test to validate that the request is processed successfully, and a corresponding response is generated.
-  Analysis: Verify that the SOAP response has all the fields/data elements as described in Section 5.
-  Inspection: Cross check SOAP response against the VADIR DB, ensuring that the data conforms to what is present in the VADIR DB.

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
The data transfer between Chapter 33 LTS and VADIR is accomplished via a Java web service. Queries are performed against the VADIR database using appropriate uniquely identifying input, generally the individual's DoD Electronic Data Exchange Person Identifier (EDIPI).  VADIR fetches the different data sets and provides the data using the structures and field names specified in Section 5 of this document.  
Occasionally DMDC adds new codes and send them to VADIR. These new codes will be transferred to DGIB. However, the codes will transfer and display in the payload but the descriptions won't until the look up table is updated.

Heading: 4. Interface Verification

The following qualification methods will be used to verify that requirements have been met:  
-  Demonstration: Ensuring that the VSCH33Service?WSDL is accessible at end points as listed in Section 5, demonstrating that the service is properly deployed and accessible from requesting entities.
-  Test: Once the accessibility of endpoints is established, run through the SOAP UI test to validate that the request is processed successfully, and a corresponding response is generated.
-  Analysis: Verify that the SOAP response has all the fields/data elements as described in Section 5.
-  Inspection: Cross check SOAP response against the VADIR DB, ensuring that the data conforms to what is present in the VADIR DB.

Heading: 5.1. End Points

The following URLs identify the service in each environment:

| Dev/Test/PIT  | https://vaausvdrapppit.aac.va.gov/vdrCH33/v2/VSCH33Service?WSDL |

|----------------------|----------------------|------------------------------|

| findPeopleByCriteria | findPeopleByCriteria | findPeopleByCriteriaResponse |
| getServicePeriods    | getServicePeriods    | getServicePeriodsResponse    |

[Chunk 5]
Source: VADIR_CH33_LTS_WS_V2.1.1_ICD_20220509 — VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
Heading: 2.3. Data Transfer

The data transfer between Chapter 33 LTS and VADIR is accomplished via a Java web service. Queries are performed against the VADIR database using appropriate uniquely identifying input, generally the individual's DoD Electronic Data Exchange Person Identifier (EDIPI).  VADIR fetches the different data sets and provides the data using the structures and field names specified in Section 5 of this document.  
Occasionally DMDC adds new codes and send them to VADIR. These new codes will be transferred to DGIB. However, the codes will transfer and display in the payload but the descriptions won't until the look up table is updated.
results.answer:
Based on the provided context, the five VADIR web service operations available through **VSCH33Service?WSDL** are:

1. **getPerson** – fetches complete Person Information using EDIPI
2. **getContactInfo** – fetches complete Demographic Information using EDIPI
3. **findPeopleByCriteria** – fetches Veteran Demographic Information using identity traits (SN)
4. **getServicePeriods** – fetches ServicePeriod and education-specific Information using EDIPI
5. **getKickerInfo** – fetches KickerInfo Response Information using EDIPI

[1], [3], [4]

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
The interface utilizes a SOAP message over HTTPS with bi-directional certificate validation based on VA-issued and commercial certificates; this allows the interface to identify and authorize the server-toserver communications to a specific endpoint identified by a Uniform Resource Locator (URL). TLS provides the message's confidentiality and integrity between the endpoints.  
The Ch33 Person Service consists of the following operations:  
-  getPerson - Returns DoD biographical information.
-  getContactInfo - Returns DoD-provided mailing address and phone number.
-  findPeopleByCriteria - Returns a maximum of fifty results that meet the supplied search criteria.
-  getServicePeriods - Returns DoD military service including service periods, exclusion periods, training periods, Guard/Reserve activation periods, Career Intermission Plan (CIP) participation, Ch30 eligibility information, Ch1606 eligibility information, Military affiliation and duty status, Military Academy periods, payments and used eligibility for multiple education programs, and Purple Heart information.
-  getKickerInfo - Returns Ch30/Ch1606 kicker amounts previously awarded.

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
This interface, although initialized from Chapter 33 LTS, is controlled via the implemented control methods utilized in the VADIR web service.
The interface is designed to utilize HTTPS with bi-directional certificate validation.  
Mutual TLS Authentication is used with VA issued certificates to identify and authorize server-to-server communications to a specific endpoint identified by a URL where your services reside. TLS encrypts the request and return messages, providing confidentiality and integrity between the endpoints.  
Current security requirements include a minimum of TLSv1.1 for network transport and SHA2 encryption inside the certificates. Additional restrictions and ongoing mitigation are in place as part of the CRISP process which maintains this application at a state acceptable with VA security standards.

Payload shall be returned in no more than 2 seconds.
This data transmission interface complies with Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) Publication 199. The following confidentiality, integrity and availability categorizations have been laid out regarding the transfer and handling of data germane to Chapter 33 LTS:

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
This software interface provides the necessary data exchange between VADIR and Chapter 33 LTS to facilitate the population and display the agreed upon data feed to support data required from VADIR to Chapter 33 LTS.  
VADIR uses the Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP) protected by Transport Layer Security (TLS) between the Chapter 33 LTS and VADIR servers after mutual authentication has been established. Data transferred under this agreement includes Service Members 'military service information as well as information concerning their eligibility for, participation in, and utilization of several Education benefits which existed prior to implementation of the Post-9/11 MGIB program.  These included Chapter 30 (Active Component), Chapter 1606 (Selected Reserve), and Chapter 1607 (REAP) as well as utilization and payment information from the VBA Benefits Delivery Network (BDN).  
Details of data transferred and of the Extensible Markup Language (XML) schema file are included in this ICD and defined in section 5.
